description_long: This is an acoustic guitar with a classic dreadnought body shape, featuring a sunburst finish on the front and a rich, dark wood grain on the back and sides. The soundhole is adorned with a decorative rosette, and the bridge and fretboard are made of a darker wood, likely rosewood or ebony. The guitar has a cutaway design, allowing easier access to higher frets, and is equipped with a built-in electronic control panel on the side, indicating it is an electro-acoustic model. The headstock is fitted with six gold tuning pegs arranged in a three-per-side configuration, and the neck appears to be constructed from a lighter wood. White binding outlines the body and neck, adding a refined visual contrast. The overall craftsmanship and finish suggest a high-quality instrument suitable for both street performances and professional use.
description: A sunburst-finished acoustic guitar with a cutaway body, decorative rosette, and built-in electronic controls, featuring gold tuning pegs and white binding.
description_view_0: Front view showing the sunburst finish, soundhole with rosette, bridge, fretboard, and strings.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the guitar's depth, cutaway design, and the electronic control panel.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the dark wood grain back, white binding, and the back of the headstock with tuning pegs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the smooth wood finish and the absence of the control panel.
attribute_headstock_design: Standard acoustic headstock with three gold tuning pegs per side, no visible logo, and a rounded contour.
